categories.title: Best Original Screenplay
categories.blurb: The best writing and storytelling in a build log.
categories.presenter: For a season-long story with real voice, real stakes, and a surprise guest narrator…

winner.threadTitle: FRC 694 StuyPulse | 2026 Build Thread | Open Alliance
winner.citation: A build thread with a whole cast of voices — including the head coach stepping up to the mic when the students left him with nothing to complain about.

quote.text: Hello friends of Stuy Robotics! For those who don't know me, I'm Joe Blay and I am the head coach of the Stuyvesant Robotics program. With all the students doing such a good job posting on OA regularly, there is nothing for me to post about.
quote.author: jblay694

nominees.citation: Turned 'shooting on the move' into a page-turner — "it's not just hard, it's writing-your-own-physics-engine hard."
A genuinely reflective Year-1 retrospective written for mentors wrestling with the same problems.
The 'Let's Talk About…' essays read like a column you'd subscribe to.

categories.title: Best Engineering Deep-Dive
categories.blurb: The most impressive technical breakdown of the season.
categories.presenter: For the team that didn't just build it, but showed their work…

winner.citation: Turned a brownout post-mortem into a free engineering lecture, complete with the math to back it up.

quote.text: Browning out is when a robot's system voltage drops to a level where the Rio cuts off all outputs. The level is adjustable, I will use 7V for this discussion.
quote.author: JamesCH95

nominees.citation: Derived shooting-on-the-move from first principles instead of hand-waving it.
A turret breakdown deep enough that the student handed the calculus off to a mentor.
The CANJunction write-up doubles as a CAN-debugging masterclass.

categories.title: Best Cinematography
categories.blurb: The best use of photos, CAD renders, and video.
categories.presenter: Roll the tape — and they had a lot of tape to roll…

winner.citation: Thirty-eight videos in a single build thread — this wasn't a blog, it was a season-long documentary.

quote.text: We've made massive progress on our design, and have even started some construction! Here's our completed full-robot CAD, up to this point.
quote.author: Crow (She/They)

nominees.threadTitle: FRC 1701 Robocubs | 2026 Build Thread | Open Alliance
nominees.citation: Over 1,400 images across the thread — a frame for every moment of the build.

nominees.threadTitle: 2358 Bearbotics | Build Blog 2026 Rebuilt | Open Alliance
nominees.citation: Let the footage tell the story when the Geneva mechanism finally came alive.

nominees.threadTitle: Tronic Titans 3161 Build Thread | Open Alliance 2026
nominees.citation: Paired clean renders with video to make every update easy to follow.

categories.title: Best Comedic Moment
categories.blurb: The funniest, most good-natured moment of the season.
categories.presenter: And the award for making the whole thread laugh without ever punching down…

winner.citation: A perfectly timed mid-post hand-off from student to mentor that earned its laugh honestly.

quote.text: It involved a lot of complex math and derivatives and stuff (I haven't done calculus yet :3), so I'll let the mentor explain later on this thread. Hi! It's me, "other mentor."
quote.author: apinguen

nominees.citation: "Sometimes you just have to steal from the best" — a cheerfully honest homage to 254's Misfire.

nominees.threadTitle: 9062 Critical Circuits | 2026 Build Thread & Robots Done Quick
nominees.citation: Ran a 'Robots Done Quick' bit and fully committed to the speedrun energy.
"I promise I won't make any more puns" (they made more puns).

categories.title: Best Comeback
categories.presenter: For the team that took the rough first act and rewrote the ending…

winner.citation: Went from 'cons: just about everything else' at Week 0 to the best event of their lives by DCMP — that's the arc.

quote.text: DCMP was the most fun and exhilarating event we've competed at ever - and probably will remain that for a long time to come.
quote.author: Vishnu Bharath

nominees.citation: "We did what we could with what we had" — and came back with a plan.

nominees.threadTitle: FRC 2826 Wave Robotics 🌊 | 2026 Open Alliance Build Thread
nominees.citation: Fought the triple-coax turret for weeks until the night it finally spun up.

nominees.threadTitle: FRC 157 The AZTECHS | 2026 Build Thread | Open Alliance
nominees.citation: The YAMS-ening: dragged a stubborn system from sim to a working bot.

categories.title: Best Supporting Team
categories.blurb: The team that gave most generously back to the community.
categories.presenter: For the team whose best work this season was making everyone else's robot better…

winner.citation: Didn't just solve their own CAN headaches — battle-tested the fix across connector types so other teams wouldn't have to.

quote.text: We currently use the Molex SL compatible version, but have spent some time battle-testing both the WAGO Lever-Lock & Direct Solder versions of this board as well to ensure proper function for teams!
quote.author: credard

nominees.threadTitle: 4561 The TerrorBytes | 2026 Build Thread | Open Alliance
nominees.citation: Published a full sponsorship guide so other teams could fund themselves.

nominees.threadTitle: FRC 7407 Wired Boars | 2026 Build Thread | Open Alliance
nominees.citation: Shared hopper simulations openly and credited the teams they learned from.
Posted their full kickoff game analysis for anyone trying to understand Rebuilt.

superlatives.title: Burning the Midnight Oil
superlatives.line: 74 posts dropped in the small hours.

superlatives.title: The Long Haul
superlatives.line: Kept the thread alive 261 days, start to finish.

superlatives.title: Fashionably Late
superlatives.line: Made a grand entrance on May 8.

superlatives.title: Hot Start
superlatives.line: 123 likes in the first week alone.

superlatives.title: The Renderer
superlatives.line: 40 images in the opening post — a visual feast.
